title: S. cerevisiae Δpho84 Complementation: Growth Curves
author: David S Milner
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kw5cxg6

steps.title: Synthesise ORF, codon-optimised for expression in...
steps.instruction: Synthesise ORF, codon-optimised for expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae , with a C-terminal His 6 tag and clone into p416 GPD under the control of the constitutive GPD promoter (pXXX).

steps.title: Transform S
steps.instruction: Transform S. cerevisiae strain BY2959 ( pho84 ::HIS3) with either p416 GPD or pXXX

steps.title: Transform BY5676 with p416 GPD (this...
steps.instruction: Transform BY5676 with p416 GPD (this will be your positive control).

steps.title: Grow cells at 30°C, 200 rpm...
steps.instruction: Grow cells at 30°C, 200 rpm shaking for 16 hours in Sc-ura medium (0.79% yeast nitrogen base without (w/o) amino acids (Formedium), 700 mg L -1 complete supplement mix - ura (Formedium), 2% glucose). N.B. Grow each strain in triplicate (3 independently obtained transformants).

steps.title: Centrifuge cells at 1,810 x g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cells at 1,810 x g for 3 minutes, and washed twice with low-phosphate (LPi) medium*, pH 4.5. *Rubin, G.M., Three forms of the 5.8-S ribosomal RNA species in Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Eur J Biochem, 1974. 41 (1): p. 197-202.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in LPi medium and...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in LPi medium and dilute to an OD 600 of 0.1.

steps.title: Inoculate 100 µl into a 96 well plate
steps.instruction: Inoculate 100 µl into a 96 well plate.

steps.title: Grow cells at 30°C in a...
steps.instruction: Grow cells at 30°C in a Fluostar Omega Lite microplate reader (BMG Labtech Ltd.). Assess OD 595 at 10-minute intervals, using continuous double-orbital shaking (200 rpm) between reads.
